Look, I know how you feel about role playing. Everybody thinks it's a waste of time, except the pros. The pros are all like, I got to role play. I got to practice. I got to run a drill. I got to play it out first. I got to run the drill, play it out. I got to feel the field. I got to know what's going to happen. In fact, role playing can increase retention of a topic by 58%. Do you know most sales training and most training in books, or 82% of the content is forgotten within 30 days? If not more than that, some reports say that it's 95% is forgotten. You know why? Because people don't role play. They don't drill it. They don't play it out. They don't work with it. I show you a baseball. This is a baseball, okay? Well, you need to work it out. I got to throw that ball. Role playing with that ball, practice drilling, rehearsing, actual practicals with that baseball is going to develop muscle. When I start developing muscle by throwing that ball, what happens? My arm changes. When my arm changes, that drill changes. So you need to drill the same thing with your phone scripts. As I start drilling that script down in a role play, I'm going to get stronger. I'm going to get more confident. I'm going to start getting a little arrogant. I'm going to start getting a little loose. That's when I need to start adapting back to my consistent and stable. Drill it down so deep that you're not thinking anymore. Okay? That if someone woke you up at two in the morning and a bomb went off, you could sit up and spit your script without even thinking about it. No matter what's going on, babies crying, internet crashing, lost your list, forgot who you were talking to, I've had all those situations happen to me. All right? In all these segments that I'm sharing with you, all this is happening to me. I need to get my script grooved in and I need to role play. This is what made me a great salesperson. Okay? The theme that will keep me from being a great person on radio or TV is my unwillingness to practice, drill and rehearse. Now I found a few things that make role playing very effective and worthy investment of my time. I use a mirror to watch myself when I deliver a script so I can watch for my confidence. I can look at my tone. I can look at my conviction. I can look at that. Do I even have that thing? I'm looking for the deal. Right? I'm looking in the mirror, watching myself make the call. Am I looking for a deal or am I just role playing? My people in my office in Miami are required to role play. I'll walk in sometime. This isn't a real role play. The customer would never say that. What are you guys doing? Don't just fall into this false sense of I'm doing this drill. Play it out like it's game day. Record yourself on video in front of a mirror. Look at feedback. Look, the bottom line is no profession, no profession. There is no profession on this planet that would get onto a court or a field or a stage without warming up. And if you think it's any difference in sales, you're arrogant and you're selfish. It's ridiculous that you wouldn't role play. It is ridiculous and expensive mistake that you would practice on customers that have money. Role play your script daily, not once a week, every day. Warm the script up. It takes about 12 hours for somebody to get rusty. That means if you went home and slept and watched any amount of TV at all, you're rusty the next day. I assure you role playing will increase your ability to convert and it's going to make you feel better about your job and it's going to increase the amount of people you actually call.
